pullquote.rule_applied: Pullquote-provenance rule (2026-05-22) — no Meeting Intelligence quote available for Georgia-specific worker classification analysis; anonymised to function-line per skill spec; no fabricated cite line.

editor_notes: Drafted using V2 editorial standard (Stripe register, capsule = stake/size/ack/promise, ONE named Georgia scenario per section — Mason/Lily/Carter, body prose has zero statutes in California-style capsule paragraphs; statutes confined to source columns of tables). Linter (verify-page.py) treated as deterministic Stage-6 substitute for sprint mode.

high_concern_flags.detail: Page guidance flags HB 389 narrowed contractor classification for delivery drivers in 2022. Page asks readers to verify whether the rule has been expanded since. Page does not assert a specific post-2022 expansion. Recommend confirmation against current Georgia DOL HB 389 guidance and any 2023-2026 amending legislation before public launch.

Task prompt references O.C.G.A. § 34-8-256 for back UI taxes + interest + civil penalties. Page cites this statute consistently in the cost table. Recommend verification of the specific O.C.G.A. § 34-8-256 text against the current 2026 statute compilation for the exact civil-penalty language.

spec_notes: Modelled on Florida sibling structure (closest common-law state) with Georgia-specific data swaps and Georgia statutes (O.C.G.A.) substituted throughout.
Common-law test treatment quoted from O.C.G.A. Title 34 Chapter 8 (SUTA) and O.C.G.A. § 34-9-2.2 (workers' comp).
Workers' comp threshold (3 employees) per O.C.G.A. § 34-9-2.
Section 530 federal safe harbour treatment distinguished from California ABC (where no equivalent state safe harbour exists).
HB 389 (2022) delivery-driver carve-out treated as narrow industry overlay; page asks reader to confirm current status with Georgia DOL.
Three named Georgia scenarios (Mason — Atlanta dev contractor; Lily — Savannah freelance designer; Carter — Augusta ops contractor) per V2 spec.
Capsule = 4-paragraph stake/size/ack/promise. Body prose statute-free per V2; statutes confined to Source columns of data tables.
End-of-page disclaimer per task brief exact specification.

stage_provenance.research: Used compliance cache georgia.json plus task-brief guidance for O.C.G.A. Title 34 Chapter 8 (SUTA), O.C.G.A. § 34-9-2.2 (workers' comp right-of-control), HB 389 delivery-driver overlay, O.C.G.A. § 34-8-256 (penalty). Cross-checked against Florida sibling (closest common-law state) and California sibling (ABC contrast) already documented.
stage_provenance.brief: Sprint mode — generated directly in deploy directory without separate brief.md. Autopilot mode, no human approval pause.
stage_provenance.draft: Single-pass mirror of Florida sibling structure with Georgia-specific content swapped throughout. V2 editorial standard applied at draft time.
stage_provenance.images: Hero reused from Georgia sibling (state-income page). Inline stamp-block visual composed in-HTML per CSS classes already in _shared/child-base.css. No gpt-image-1 calls.
stage_provenance.internal_links: 8 live + 5 plain-text wave-N per 4-state link gate.
